svg.title: NexFlow microservices architecture diagram
svg.desc: System architecture showing the API Gateway receiving requests from the Frontend and routing them to six backend microservices, each with its own database, all communicating asynchronously through RabbitMQ.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me about the NexFlow frontend')
sendPrompt('How does the NexFlow API Gateway work?')
g.text: JWT auth · request routing · CORS · port 8080

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Tell me about the Customer Service in NexFlow')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the Resource Service in NexFlow')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the Booking Service in NexFlow')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the Payment Service in NexFlow')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the Notification Service in NexFlow')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the Tracking Service in NexFlow')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the customer database schema')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the resource database schema')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the booking database schema')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the payment database schema')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the notification database schema')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the tracking database schema')

svg.text: async event bus (RabbitMQ topic exchange: nexflow_events)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does RabbitMQ work in NexFlow?')

svg.text: Redis :6379 (session cache) · Location Simulator (dev tool, auto-moves carrier positions)
